summary: Good‑quality matrix recording of a legendary 1980 show with standout performances on Greatest Story Ever Told, Mexicali Blues and more
review: The tape captures a historic late‑‘80 pivot show that fans revere for its high energy and adventurous setlist. Reviewers praise a slew of highlights – from the soaring "Greatest Story Ever Told" to the fiery "Mexicali Blues" and a superb "Friend of the Devil" – while noting the recording is a clear matrix source that still needs slight pitch correction.

ai_rating.rationale: The show receives enthusiastic praise with many standout songs and only minor performance dips; the recording is judged clear and listenable despite small speed/pitch issues, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple audience/soundboard sources were stitched together; the best 2nd‑set matrix (nak700_wise_miller_clugston) is clear but runs slightly fast and needs a +2 % pitch correction for the SBD portion. Some versions are muddy or run too fast, but overall the image is solid.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set maintains a high, sustained level of playing with several explosive moments; the second set is a bit more uneven but offers adventurous segues and risks that hint at the band’s upcoming evolution.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly lauded – wrangling Mexicali Blues, bursting on Friend of the Devil, and delivering brilliant lead work throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocals slip on "Saturday Night" (he loses his voice), but his rhythm playing still supports the band.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (Keith) and Jerry lock in tightly on Far From Me; overall keyboard contributions are noted as right‑on‑the‑dime for several tunes.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers deliberately pull out during "Space" to create a head/body tweak, adding a playful texture.
